llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm exists: ai_assisted, which pools all thirteen AI models of differing compute sizes. This is compared against the control arm with no AI assistance.
llm_extracted.notes: Sample sizes (n_human and n_ai) are not provided in the arms data. The primary comparison shows a 31.1% reduction in task completion time with AI assistance (600.7 seconds without AI vs 413.8 seconds with AI, p=0.000). Since time is the outcome and smaller is better, the percent change is negative (-0.311). Additional statistics about scaling relationships with model compute are reported but not extracted as they do not represent the primary human-only vs human+AI comparison.
source_quotes: The average time taken by participants to conduct a task without access to any AI model was almost exactly ten minutes (600.7 seconds). With access to any AI model this was reduced to 413.8 seconds (p=0.000), a statistically significant reduction in time of 31.1%.
